method: Two independent AI-proxy label passes over the per-claim judge traces from the committed seed-baseline.json (median run). Labeler A is claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 given a strict prompt: 'Only label as supported if the claim is explicitly stated or directly inferable without any extrapolation from the provided context.' Labeler B is claude-sonnet-4-6 given a lenient prompt: 'Label as supported if the claim is explicitly stated or reasonably inferable from the provided context.' Both labelers received identical inputs (claim text + grounding context) independently with no access to each other's labels or the judge verdicts. Grounding context was identical to seed-baseline.json: k=6 retrieved chunks for retrieve cases, full stuffed record for stuff cases. Held-out cases include paired Haiku- and Sonnet-generated outputs on shared queries to support self-preference measurement. Generated 2026-06-04. LIMITATION: AI-proxy labelers approximate the structure of human labeling for this build; true calibration requires human annotators with clinical background. Small-n (86 claims across 11 outputs) means all kappa values are directional only.

seedCases.note: 37 claims from median judge run; all judge-supported. Labeler B marks claim 27 (intimate-partner-abuse) unsupported: context lists it as a problem but B requires explicit verbatim phrasing absent from the extracted snippet.

seedCases.note: 3 claims, all judge-supported. Both labelers agree.

claims.claim: There are no current allergies documented in this patient record
The ALLERGIES section explicitly states: 'There are no current allergies at the time of this document's creation'
No adverse reactions are documented in any other section of the provided patient record

seedCases.note: 13 claims. Claim 9 (O2 timing) was marked 'partial' by the judge. Labeler A (strict) marks it unsupported: the date range is an interpretation not explicit in the retrieved snippet. Labeler B (lenient) accepts the inference.

seedCases.note: 17 claims from the pre-authored hallucinated output; all judge-unsupported. Claim 2 ('The patient has type 2 diabetes mellitus') is marked supported by Labeler B: Brenna468's record may contain diabetes-related entries; B infers the claim is plausible even if not verbatim. Labeler A marks it unsupported: the record states no current allergies but does not explicitly list T2DM in the stuffed context.

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on medications query for Brenna468. Short, direct. All 4 claims grounded verbatim in the record.

claims.claim: The provided patient record contains no documented current medications

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same medications query; 2x longer. Claim 2 asserts no documented medication changes/discontinuations — this is not explicitly stated in the record, only the absence of active medications is stated.

claims.claim: Brenna468 has no active medications documented in the provided record
The medications section confirms no medications are active as of document creation
There are no documented medication changes or discontinuations in this clinical record
